query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Desecration', 'An act of disrespect towards something considered sacred', 'Her desecration became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desert', 'To leave, to abandon, to forsake', 'Over time, the community learned to desert its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deserve', 'Earn or merit', 'Scholars noted a striking deserve between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desiccant', 'Causing dryness', 'Scholars noted a striking desiccant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desiccate', 'Lacking interest, passion, or energy', 'The author hoped the new policy would desiccate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Designate', 'To officially assign a specified status or name to something or someone', 'The author hoped the new policy would designate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desist', 'To cease to proceed or act, to stop', 'The author hoped the new policy would desist tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desistance', 'The act or state of desisting, cessation', 'Scholars noted a striking desistance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desolate', 'Barren or laid waste', 'The author hoped the new policy would desolate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Despair', 'To give up as beyond hope or expectation, to despair of', 'Critics argued that the plan might despair the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desperado', 'A bold outlaw, especially one from the southern wild west', 'Her desperado became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desperate', 'Being filled with, or in a state of despair, hopeless', 'Critics argued that the plan might desperate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Despicable', 'Fit or deserving to be despised, contemptible', 'Critics argued that the plan might despicable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Despite', 'In spite of', 'Scholars noted a striking despite between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Despond', 'To give up the will, courage, or spirit', 'The author hoped the new policy would despond tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Despondent', 'In low spirits from loss of hope', 'The reviewer described the tone as despondent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Despot', 'A ruler with absolute power', 'Scholars noted a striking despot between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Despotic', 'Characteristic of an absolute ruler or absolute rule', 'The essay highlights the despotic of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Despotism', 'An absolute or autocratic government', 'Scholars noted a striking despotism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Destitute', 'Without the basic necessities of life', 'The essay highlights the destitute of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Destitution', 'The action of deserting or abandoning', 'Scholars noted a striking destitution between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desuetude', 'Disuse, obsolescence', 'Her desuetude became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desultory', 'Lacking a clear plan or purpose', '“His desultory speech confused the audience.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deter', 'To discourage someone from doing something', 'Critics argued that the plan might deter the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deteriorate', 'To make worse, to make inferior in quality or value', 'Over time, the community learned to deteriorate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Determinate', 'Having exact and discernible limits or form', 'The passage presents a determinate portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Determination', 'Firmness of purpose, resoluteness', 'Scholars noted a striking determination between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deterrent', 'A thing that is intended to discourage someone from some act', 'Critics argued that the plan might deterrent the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detest', 'To dislike intensely', 'The author hoped the new policy would detest tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detonate', 'To explode or cause to explode', 'Over time, the community learned to detonate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detract', 'To diminish or take away from the value, importance, or quality of something', 'Over time, the community learned to detract its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detraction', 'The act of taking away', 'Scholars noted a striking detraction between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detriment', 'The state if being harmed or damaged', 'Her detriment became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detrimental', 'Causing harm or damage', '“Excessive screen time can be detrimental to eye health.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detritus', 'Waste or debris of any kind', 'Scholars noted a striking detritus between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detrude', 'To push downwards with force', 'The author hoped the new policy would detrude tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Detumescence', 'The process of subsiding from a state of tension', 'Her detumescence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Devastate', 'To destroy or ruin (something)', 'Over time, the community learned to devastate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Develop', 'To grow or cause to grow and become more mature', 'Critics argued that the plan might develop the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deviance', 'The state of departing from usual or accepted standards', 'Her deviance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deviate', 'To go off course from, to change course, to change plans', 'Over time, the community learned to deviate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Devilry', 'Wicked activity', 'The essay highlights the devilry of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deviltry', 'Extreme cruelty', 'Scholars noted a striking deviltry between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Devious', 'Showing skillful use of underhanded tactics to achieve goals', 'Critics argued that the plan might devious the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Devise', 'To use one''s intellect to plan or design (something)', 'Over time, the community learned to devise its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Devitalize', 'To deprive of vitality, make lifeless, weaken', 'Critics argued that the plan might devitalize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Devoid', 'Empty, having none of, completely without', 'Scholars noted a striking devoid between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Devour', 'To eat (food or prey) hungrily or quickly', 'The author hoped the new policy would devour tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Devout', 'Devoted to religion or to religious feelings and duties', 'Critics argued that the plan might devout the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dexterity', 'Skill in performing tasks, especially with the hands', 'Scholars noted a striking dexterity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
